mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions signal strong aggregate demand and wage growth, triggering a positive risk-on sentiment that lifts S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and residential housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion. This joint signal reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding the long-term trend indicates capacity expansion that suppresses input cost inflation. This supply-side relief improves corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 210 signal entrenched inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL stays above 210, or the signal exhibits no directional predictability after adjusting for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening. This dual confirmation accelerates consumer spending expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns are indistinguishable from baseline when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances indicate expanding household leverage and consumption capacity. This demand surge lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon, provided credit growth remains below historical crisis thresholds.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 15% year-over-year, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of financial stress when credit spreads widen.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth by more than 0.5 percentage points, supply-side bottlenecks ease faster than demand weakens. This favorable margin environment triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the spread between INDPRO and GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with forward equity returns in a 3-year backtest.
